title: pentanema sabuletorum
intro: Pentanema sabuletorum, also known as hairy knapweed or coastal knapweed, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to the coastal regions of Europe and North Africa. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and ease digestive discomfort. The health benefits of Pentanema sabuletorum include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
